text.title: sql-optimization Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for sql-optimization as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: SELECT * — fetching all columns when only a subset is needed.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Fetches unused data across the network and prevents covering index optimizations.

failure-mode.description: LIKE '%pattern%' with a leading wildcard on an indexed column.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Leading wildcards prevent B-tree index use; the planner falls back to a sequential scan. Use a full-text search index (GIN with tsvector) for substring search.

failure-mode.description: ORDER BY RAND() or ORDER BY random() on large tables.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Assigns a random value to every row, sorts all rows, then discards most. O(N log N) for every page load. Use reservoir sampling or a random offset instead.

failure-mode.description: Implicit type conversion in WHERE: WHERE user_id = 123 when user_id is VARCHAR.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: The planner casts every indexed column value to match the literal's type, preventing index use. Match literal types to column types.

failure-mode.description: UPDATE or DELETE without a WHERE clause.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Modifies every row in the table. In production this is almost always a mistake and can cause irreversible data loss.

failure-mode.description: DISTINCT as a workaround for duplicate rows in a JOIN result.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: DISTINCT symptoms indicate a JOIN that produces a Cartesian product — usually a missing join condition. Fix the query; do not mask the problem with DISTINCT.

failure-mode.description: Applying a function to an indexed column in WHERE (e.g. WHERE DATE(created_at) = '2024-01-15').
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: The function wraps every stored value, bypassing the B-tree index. Use range conditions instead: WHERE created_at >= '2024-01-15' AND created_at < '2024-01-16'.

failure-mode.description: Not using prepared statements for repeated parameterized queries.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Non-prepared queries re-parse and re-plan on every execution. Prepared statements cache the plan, improve performance, and prevent SQL injection.
